title: Implement shared secret-scan script (guard + lefthook callers)
description: From layer-composition decision. One secret-scan script invoked by both the PreToolUse agent guard and lefthook pre-commit. Target-aware: blocks display/search of secret paths (.env*, *.pem, *.key, credentials, *.tfstate) and unfiltered env dumps. Configurable path list at top.

title: Verify beads git hooks survive lefthook install (chain mode)
description: Verification item from design layer-composition section + ADR-0003. lefthook must install in non-clobber/chain mode after bd init and still call beads' pre-existing hooks. lefthook's call-existing-hooks behavior varies by hook type — confirm empirically before relying on it.

title: Define canonical allowlist + deny-floor seed contents
description: Open item from design §9. Enumerate the embedded canonical allow globs (bd, instill, git read/write, grep/rg/find, mise, gw, slack-cli, rtk — broad prefix per ADR-0002) and the deny floor D1-D10 (incl. secret-exposure D9/D10). This is the 'day one it just works' contents that the reconciler ships.
notes:
Seed contents decided 2026-06-18 (grill-with-docs). Full spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-18-allowlist-deny-floor-seed.md

Key decisions:
- Glob format Bash(cmd:*) colon form; param-form Bash(command:rm *) is silently ignored by Claude Code.
- Compounds auto-approve NATIVELY (matcher is shell-operator-aware) — reverses ADR-0002 premise; seed job is just to make allowlist complete.
- Allowlist = universal dev baseline + per-stack language slice + tagged-optional personal section (stripped unless --include-personal).
- Decided IN: git rm/mv, bash:*, sed/awk. Decided OUT (denied): curl/wget (only exfil control left; use WebFetch).
- Secret protection = whole-command-line path-TOKEN scan (port guardrails-bin), NOT command-name list (research-confirmed bypassable).
- 4 defense layers seeded: (1) native Read/Edit/Write secret matchers, (2) path-token guard hook, (3) OS sandbox BOTH agents (portable: Claude settings.json + Codex config.toml), (4) interpreter-class deny.
- Sandbox: network ON, FS-isolation-only (network-off breaks push/installs); egress controlled by guard D11 exfil deny.
- Deny floor expanded to D1-D16 (added env-dump, exfil, system/process, docker, history, interpreter).
close_reason: Seed contents fully specified in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-18-allowlist-deny-floor-seed.md; ADR-0002 amended for research findings.

title: Author mise.toml template ([tools]+[tasks]) per language
description: From ADR-0003. One mise.toml per stack: [tools] pins toolchain+lefthook, [tasks] defines lint/test/fmt/ci. Single bootstrap (mise install); lefthook + CI both call mise run. Per-language gate commands sourced from guideline files.

title: Build allowlist reconciler (mkproj sync-allowlist) + SessionStart notify
description: From ADR-0001. Managed-block rewrite from embedded canonical source; SessionStart hook (Claude + Codex) detects staleness and notifies (never auto-mutates). Versioned <!-- BEGIN MKPROJ ALLOW v:N --> block.

title: Write mkproj design spec doc
description: Capture the approved brainstorming design for the mkproj scaffolding system into docs/superpowers/specs/.

title: Write typescript.md / rust.md / bash.md guideline files
description: Prerequisite to expanding template catalog beyond Go/Python/C# (v1 scope decided in ADR-0003). Each guideline file unlocks its language's templates under the 'guidelines = source of truth' invariant.
